macro_segments.title: The Case of Shetita (Roasted Grain Porridge) and Rabbinic Decrees Against Mixture

micro_segments.title: Definition and Preparation of Shetita with Wheat, Barley, and Lentils
Dispute Between Rav, Levi, and Avuha d'Shmuel on Permissibility of Lentil Shetita
Cases of Agreement and Disagreement: Wheat/Barley Permitted, Lentil with Vinegar Prohibited, Lentil with Water Disputed
Rabbinic Decree (Gezeira) to Prohibit Water-Based Lentil Shetita to Prevent Mixture with Vinegar Version
Rav's Refusal to Extend Gezeira to Wheat and Barley Shetita; Practical Acceptance in Nehardea
Talmudic Sources: Barzilai's Gift to David of Two Types of Shetita

macro_segments.title: Pickled Vegetables and Wine Vinegar: Distinction Between Removing Bad Taste and Adding Good Taste

micro_segments.title: Chazekiah's Rule: Pickles with Wine Only Prohibited If It is Customary; Known Cases Prohibited Even for Benefit
Distinction Between Muryas (Fish Brine) and Pickles: Wine to Remove Stink vs. Wine to Sweeten
Rabbi Yochanan's Lenient View: Even Known Wine in Pickles is Permitted
Rashi's Explanation: Mamash of Wine Not Consumed in Pickles Since Brine Not Drunk

macro_segments.title: Kosher Fish Identification: Scales and Fins as Signs of Kashrut

micro_segments.title: Introduction to Torah's Two Signs of Kosher Fish: Fin (Snapir) and Scale (Kaskeset)
Talmudic Principle: All Fish with Scales Have Fins; Only Scales Are Determinative
Risk of Non-Kosher Fish Mixture in Commercial Fishing and Need for Skin Identification
Fish Development: Sultanit and Afitz Develop Scales Later in Life; Akunas and Others Lose Scales When Removed from Water
Modern Fish Identification: Tuna, Swordfish, and Sturgeon as Cases of Disputed Kashrut

macro_segments.title: Fish Products and Sources: Organs, Roe, and River-Specific Fish Populations

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Abbahu's Teaching: Fish Organs and Roe Can Be Purchased from Any Vendor
Presumption Regarding Roe (Bara): Presupposed to Come Only from Kosher-Fish Regions (Pulsa and Spain)
Abaye's Teaching on Bab River: All Fish Are Kosher Due to Strong Current, Salty Water, and Mud Preventing Non-Kosher Spawning
Ravina's Correction: Bab River No Longer Exclusively Kosher After Canals Mixed Water from Other Rivers
Named Kosher and Non-Kosher Fish: Donkey of the Sea (Permitted), Ox of the Sea (Prohibited), Shafarnuna (Permitted), Kadesh Nuna (Prohibited)

macro_segments.title: Case Studies in Fish Identification by Sages: Testing Questionable Fish for Kosher Signs

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Akiva in Ginzak: Fish Resembling Non-Kosher Hipusa Determined Kosher Upon Finding Scales
Rav Ashi in Tamduria: Fish Resembling Non-Kosher Tslufcha Determined Kosher Upon Inspection
Rabbah bar bar Chana: Recognition of Non-Kosher Fish Brine by Identifying Non-Kosher Fish Name (Bati)

macro_segments.title: Prohibition on Asafoetida (Chiltit) Cut with Non-Kosher Knife: Spice Reviving Knife Fat Taste

micro_segments.title: Asafoetida (Chiltit) Slice Prohibited Despite Knife-Transferred Taste Because Spice Revives Knife Fat as Beneficial Taste

macro_segments.title: Trust and Presumption in Kosher Food: Slaves, Wives, and Household Members of the Reliable

micro_segments.title: Principle: Slave of a Chaver (Reliable Person) Is Considered Like a Chaver
Presumption After Death: Widow, Children, and Household of Deceased Chaver Retain Kosher Status Until Suspected Otherwise
Baraita: Wife Marrying a Chaver Needs Formal Acceptance; Wife Leaving Chaver for Non-Chaver (Debate Between Rabbi Meir and Rabbi Yehuda)
Rabbi Shimon ben Elazar's Case: Woman Assisting Husband (Chaver or Tax Collector) Reflects Her Acceptance of His Household Standards

macro_segments.title: Seals and Standards of Kashrut Certification: Double vs. Single Seal Requirements by Product

micro_segments.title: Mnemonic Chavit (Milk, Meat, Wine, Techelit): Four Products Requiring Double Seal of Kashrut
Mnemonic Champag (Chiltit, Muryas, Pat, Gevina): Four Products Permitted with Single Seal
Bread (Pat) Not Concerned Due to Detectability of Freshness and Grain Type Differences
Milk Requires Two Seals While Cheese Only Needs One: Parallel to Skinned Fish (Cannot Determine Kosher Species)
Shmuel's Mnemonic (Bayit Instead of Chavit): Excludes Bread; Parallel to Skinned Fish Concern

macro_segments.title: Six Products in Syria: Restricted Purchases to Reputable Dealers Except When Guest in Private Home

micro_segments.title: Six Products (Wine, Muryas, Milk, Melach Sankundit, Chiltit, Gevina) Cannot Be Purchased in Syria Except from Reputable Dealer
Exception: All Six Products Permitted If One Is Guest in a Private Home Due to Ba'al Habayit's Presumed Kashrut Observance
Rabbi Yehoshua ben Levi's Principle: Host Will Not Serve Non-Kosher to Guest; Will Serve What He Eats Himself
